fact: February 20 is exactly 51 days into the year, which means there are still 314 days left to go!
In the Northern Hemisphere, this is when maple trees start getting ready to make syrup - their sap begins flowing faster as temperatures swing between freezing at night and warmer during the day.
The sun rises about 2 minutes earlier each day around February 20 in most places, so mornings are getting noticeably brighter.
February 20 always falls under the Pisces zodiac sign - the fish constellation that's supposed to make people extra dreamy and creative.
In Australia and other Southern Hemisphere countries, February 20 is peak summer heat season, when some desert areas can hit 120°F during the day but drop 40 degrees at night.
Earthworms start wiggling closer to the surface around this date in temperate climates because the soil is finally warming up after winter.
February 20 sits exactly 28 days before the spring equinox, making it the perfect time to spot winter constellations like Orion before they disappear for the season.
In northern regions, February 20 typically marks the time when snow starts making that crunchy sound when you walk on it because of specific temperature and humidity conditions.
Dandelions begin sprouting their first tiny leaves around this date, even when there's still snow on the ground - they're basically nature's rebels.
The moon phase on February 20 changes every year, but when it's a full moon on this date, it appears extra bright because winter air is clearer and less humid.
